subject: Gaming
title: Crunch Culture: The Human Cost
description: A quick, practical guide to Crunch Culture: The Human Cost.

hook.text: Developers work 80-hour weeks for months before launch. Crunch culture destroys health for entertainment.

buildup.text: EA's 'EA Spouse' scandal in 2004 exposed forced overtime. Workers described it as months of mandatory crunch.

discovery.text: Rockstar, CD Projekt Red, and Naughty Dog all faced crunch scandals. The pattern repeats across the industry.

twist.text: Players demand bigger games faster. That pressure flows directly to developers working unsustainable hours.

climax.text: Unionization efforts are growing. Developers are organizing to fight for sustainable working conditions.

punchline.text: Your favorite game cost someone their health. Crunch has a human price.

quiz.question: What is crunch culture in game development?
options: Extended periods of mandatory overtime, often 80+ hour weeks
A type of game compression technology
